win_draw_loss.reason: Portugal have the stronger squad and midfield control, but verified Sporttery prices and starting XIs were unavailable.

handicap.reason: Congo DR's defensive core and counterattacking forwards make a one-goal Portugal win plausible.

correct_score.reason: Best fit for Portugal control plus a reduced Congo DR chance volume.
Congo DR have enough forward quality through Wissa and Bakambu to punish one transition.
Covers a slower opener if Congo DR's low block holds for long periods.

Requires an earlier Portugal breakthrough or a Congo DR counter goal.

fixture.notes: Houston local reporting references the Portugal vs Congo DR match at NRG Stadium with a noon kickoff on 2026-06-17.

sporttery.note: No verified public China Sporttery price was available from this run.

comparison_odds.note: Group outrights only, not match odds or Sporttery.

team_news: Cristiano Ronaldo has been cleared to play Portugal's opener and is chasing a sixth World Cup scoring campaign.
Portugal's creative core includes Bruno Fernandes, Vitinha, Bernardo Silva and Joao Neves.
Congo DR rely on Axel Tuanzebe, Chancel Mbemba and Aaron Wan-Bissaka in defense, with Yoane Wissa and Cedric Bakambu as key attacking outlets.
weather_venue: Houston local reports flag heat index around 94F, heavy rain, possible severe thunderstorms and flash-flood risk on match day.
NRG Stadium has a retractable roof, reducing direct wind/rain impact if closed.
historical_structural: Portugal are a top-six ranked side and Group K favorite.
Congo DR return to the World Cup for the first time since 1974 and have no pressure as the clear underdog.
First group match: Portugal need three points, Congo DR would value a draw.

risks: Official China Sporttery odds and single-match status unavailable.
Portugal's attacking balance can change depending on Ronaldo's role and starting XI.
Congo DR's defensive block and transition threat make deep handicap exposure risky.
Houston storm conditions can disrupt travel and preparation even if the roof is closed.

post_match_review.variance_note: Portugal scored early but lost attacking fluency; Congo DR's disciplined block and Wissa's set-piece equalizer produced the draw. The two-goal total call was correct.
